suites.scope: classify_intent primary, confidence and secondary plus vyane-service primary-intent exposure; stateful history, benchmark and feedback routing are excluded

sanitized automatic routing behavior with reference-native profile effort and no-eligible cases counted as normalized matches; Rust candidate mapping, explicit model/selector, rendered-task target selection, frontier filtering, and frozen replay remain explicit open differences

cases.blocker: BLOCKER EXE-05: the reference dispatch entry does not consume the Rust candidate/profile input shape, so this Rust default-route tuple cannot be claimed as a cross-implementation match.
BLOCKER EXE-05: explicit effort is accepted by the reference dispatch entry, but the selected target tuple depends on a Rust-only candidate/profile mapping that the reference entry does not consume.
BLOCKER EXE-05: the Rust automatic selector has no typed explicit-model override; adding one requires a bounded trusted input that is frozen across detached and daemon replay.
BLOCKER EXE-05: the reference dispatch entry has no same-shaped selector-to-profile-to-target resolution contract; Rust explicit profile selection is one-sided behavior.
BLOCKER EXE-05: both implementations route rendered task text, but this selected target tuple depends on a Rust-only tag/candidate mapping and is not reference-equivalent evidence.
BLOCKER EXE-05: the reference route decision has no profile-failover-chain filtering contract; Rust keeps this stronger full-chain safety rule as an explicit scope difference.
BLOCKER EXE-05: the reference router has no direct provider/model failover ambiguity contract; Rust fails closed when profile provenance cannot prove a leg non-frontier.

BLOCKER EXE-06: the reference workflow replay cache has no same-shaped frozen automatic-chain contract; this case pins Rust chain reconstruction, changed-task divergence, and missing-label failure, not the later production target-snapshot fence.

suites.scope: classify_failover_reason taxonomy only; Rust failover_eligible is pinned as one-sided regression data and is not an oracle-equivalence claim; production attempt behavior is outside this suite and requires separate EXE-02 cross-repository acceptance evidence

cases.blocker: BLOCKER EXE-02/GOV-04: Rust has no dedicated policy-blocked ErrorKind; Unsupported is fail-closed but cannot be normalized as the oracle's policy_blocked reason without losing taxonomy truth.
BLOCKER EXE-02: Rust intentionally combines rate limiting and quota exhaustion in ErrorKind::RateLimited; a taxonomy decision is required before these outputs can be accepted as equal.
